guideline_references: Heart Rhythm Society 2015 Expert Consensus Statement on POTS / Inappropriate Sinus Tachycardia and Vasovagal Syncope
CHEST/Australian dysautonomia guidance on active stand and head-up tilt testing
intentional_test_features: Anchoring / attribution trap: a young, anxious, 'jittery' patient with a racing heart is easily misattributed to a panic/anxiety disorder — the case probes whether the system resists premature closure on anxiety and recognises a reproducible, gravity-locked haemodynamic pattern.

Telehealth limit as the tier driver: the diagnosis needs active stand testing plus an ECG to exclude a paroxysmal tachyarrhythmia and significant hypovolaemia — neither is obtainable over chat — so the honest AI tier is an urgent same-day in-person assessment even though the final diagnosis is benign.
Discriminates dangerous mimics: distinguishing a postural, immediately-supine-relieved tachycardia from inappropriate sinus tachycardia, orthostatic hypotension, paroxysmal SVT and dehydration.
Sex inferred (female) from epidemiology and context — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CVD (Cardiovascular long-form SOAP), source note CDV-034.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
